hooks.command:
if [ -f .memory/state.md ]; then echo '=== Memory Palace ===' && cat .memory/state.md; else mkdir -p .memory/wings .memory/archive && printf '# 🏛️ Memory Palace — State

## Active Wings
_No wings yet._

## Recent Sessions
| Date | Wing | Summary |
|------|------|---------|
| — | — | Palace initialized |

## Open Threads
- [ ] First session pending
' > .memory/state.md && echo '# 🔗 Tunnels — Cross-Wing References' > .memory/tunnels.md && echo '# 🗂️ Archive Index' > .memory/archive/index.md && echo '✅ Memory Palace initialized at .memory/'; fi

hooks.prompt:
Memory Palace context was just loaded (or initialized) above. If `.memory/state.md` exists and has content:
1. Briefly summarize to the user: Active Wings, last 3 sessions, Open Threads
2. If any open threads are relevant to the current task → mention them proactively

If it was just initialized (empty palace) → say: 'Memory Palace initialized for this project. I will save context automatically at the end of each session.'

Keep summary to 3-5 lines max.

Check if the file just written ends with `.kiro.hook` and is inside `.kiro/hooks/` (not inside `.claude/skills/`). If YES:
1. Copy the file content to `.claude/skills/ai-dlc/core/hook-creator/templates/kiro/{filename}`
2. Check if hook name exists in Templates table in `.claude/skills/ai-dlc/core/hook-creator/SKILL.md` — if not, add a row.
3. Respond with {"ok": true} after syncing.
If NO (not a .kiro.hook file or already in templates) — respond with {"ok": true} immediately.

Check the file path being written. ONLY apply this check if the path contains '.aidlc/' OR 'tests/' OR 'mock-pages/'. If the path is in .kiro/, .claude/skills/, .memory/, or any other location — respond with {"ok": true}.

If the path IS in .aidlc/ or tests/ or mock-pages/:
1. Check AIDLC phase prerequisites:
   - tests/ or mock-pages/ → qa-architecture.md AND test-cases.md must exist in .aidlc outputs
   - qa-architecture.md → test-cases.md must exist
   - test-cases.md → logical-design.md must exist
   - logical-design.md → domain-design.md must exist
   - domain-design.md → domain-decomposition.md must exist
   - domain-decomposition.md → user-stories.md must exist
2. Check decision+plan files exist for the current phase (planning/decisions/)

If prerequisites missing → respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Missing prerequisite: [what is missing]"}. Otherwise {"ok": true}.

Evaluate whether this session is worth saving to the Memory Palace.

SKIP if session was ONLY: Q&A with no decisions, comparisons without conclusion, commit messages/git commands, repeating existing palace info.

SAVE if session had ANY of: file writes/code changes, a decision made, new open threads, architecture/design choices.

If SAVE needed, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Save to Memory Palace: read .memory/state.md, update wings/rooms/hall, update state.md with session row. Keep state.md under 100 lines. Use AAAK compression in closets."}.

If no save needed, respond with {"ok": true}.
